`,(0,e.jsx)(t.p,{children:"Multi-task models are trained to learn multiple tasks simultaneously. This means we need to compute the loss for each task separately and then combine them to get an overall loss. Typically, we define a loss function for each task depending on the ML category of the task. In our case, we use a binary cross-entropy loss for each binary classification task, and a regression loss such as MAE [11], MSE [12], or Huber loss [13] for the regression task (dwell-time prediction). The overall loss is computed by combining task-specific losses, as shown in Figure 10.13."}),`
